{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope should we take to 'make this stronger'? Choose one: (A) Sentence- and paragraph-level polish only (R1.6-focused); (B) Strengthen conceptual framing and ensure internal argument/claim consistency and claim–evidence fit without adding new sections or citations (R1.1, R1.2, R1.3, plus R1.6); (C) Deeper restructure and positioning, including novelty framing and references as needed (R1.1–R1.5, R1.7, plus R1.6).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "A stronger piece typically requires clarifying and tightening the core thesis and contribution framing above the sentence level.",
    "R1.2": "Perceived strength depends on a coherent argument chain that aligns method description with stated claims and insights.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about performance and sample efficiency must be precisely matched to the reported evaluations to read as strong and credible.",
    "R1.6": "Improving clarity, concision, and flow at the sentence/paragraph level directly increases the text’s perceived strength.",
    "RX.1": "Clear acknowledgment of uncertainties and limitations prevents overstating confidence while reinforcing credibility.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding unsupported or absolute superiority claims is essential for a stronger yet accurate presentation.",
    "RX.3": "Adhering to the requested scope prevents scope creep into experimental redesign or new data collection.",
    "RX.4": "Tempering claims reduces risk of downstream misinterpretation or overreliance on the results in sensitive applications.",
    "RX.5": "Maintaining traceability between the original text and any strengthened statements preserves provenance and auditability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope should we take to 'make this stronger'? Choose one: (A) Sentence- and paragraph-level polish only (R1.6-focused); (B) Strengthen conceptual framing and ensure internal argument/claim consistency and claim–evidence fit without adding new sections or citations (R1.1, R1.2, R1.3, plus R1.6); (C) Deeper restructure and positioning, including novelty framing and references as needed (R1.1–R1.5, R1.7, plus R1.6).

rationale.R1.1: A stronger piece typically requires clarifying and tightening the core thesis and contribution framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: Perceived strength depends on a coherent argument chain that aligns method description with stated claims and insights.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about performance and sample efficiency must be precisely matched to the reported evaluations to read as strong and credible.
rationale.R1.6: Improving clarity, concision, and flow at the sentence/paragraph level directly increases the text’s perceived strength.
rationale.RX.1: Clear acknowledgment of uncertainties and limitations prevents overstating confidence while reinforcing credibility.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding unsupported or absolute superiority claims is essential for a stronger yet accurate presentation.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the requested scope prevents scope creep into experimental redesign or new data collection.
rationale.RX.4: Tempering claims reduces risk of downstream misinterpretation or overreliance on the results in sensitive applications.
rationale.RX.5: Maintaining traceability between the original text and any strengthened statements preserves provenance and auditability.
